replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Every culture has its own internal logic and social fabric that has sustained it for generations, and stepping in with an outsider's savior complex often does more harm than good. The goal of this documentary is simply to capture and respect their way of life as it is, without passing judgment. Thank you for bringing such a thoughtful and deep perspective to the discussion!

comments.author: @kennethenaholo9540
comments.text: I like this story , people's culture must be respected , the western world kill for dollars and gold also sound barbaric ,greed ,western countries has drained africa of its  resources keep them in poverty

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Thank you for sharing such a profound and heavy perspective. You’ve touched on a painful historical reality—the deep scars left by resource exploitation in Africa are undeniable. The main goal of this documentary is to shift the lens away from those outside pressures and truly honor the unique culture, resilience, and traditions of the South Sudanese people on their own terms. Really appreciate you tuning in and bringing this vital context to the conversation!

comments.author: @FunnyMr-s2d
comments.text: There is a girl can wear Rwandan Flag thank you can you wear nice Flag

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Glad you noticed that! The colors of the Rwandan flag are stunning. We’ll definitely keep featuring nice flags and local traditions from around the world in our upcoming videos. Thanks for tuning in! 🙏

comments.author: @hanabi3241
comments.text: South Sudan is a powerful reminder of how resource scarcity shapes human culture and values. What the modern world might find shocking—where cows are literally a matter of life, death, and political power—is a brilliantly adapted survival system for these pastoralist communities. Cattle are not just animals here; they are their banks, their dowries, and their entire identity. This documentary does an incredible job of showing the raw, beautiful, yet complex reality of a world completely untouched by Western consumerism

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Spot on! You’ve perfectly captured the exact message we wanted to convey. It’s so easy for outside observers to judge these traditions, but when you look at it through the lens of survival and resource scarcity, everything makes sense. Cattle really are the ultimate currency and anchor for the Mundari and Dinka people. Thank you for this deeply thoughtful and articulate comment!

replies.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your perspective. The information in the documentary was based on the accounts given by the local guides and sources we interviewed during the production. However, we know that realities can vary within different communities. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention!
